# Tokcat — extended LLM context

## Identity

Tokcat is a free, open-source, native macOS menu-bar application that visualizes AI coding token usage and cost as a recent 30-day 2D stacked token chart and a full-year 3D GitHub-style contribution graph. The project lives at https://github.com/handlecusion/tokcat and is maintained by handlecusion. License: MIT.

## Single-paragraph summary (high citability)

Tokcat is a native macOS menu-bar app, built with Tauri 2 (Rust shell + React/Vite frontend), that reads local AI coding usage logs into a glanceable, on-device dashboard for token spend. It surfaces Claude Code, OpenAI Codex CLI, Cursor IDE, OpenCode, Google Gemini CLI, GitHub Copilot CLI, Amp, Droid, Hermes, and Grok Build in an Overview dashboard with per-client tabs, shows today's tokens, USD cost, totals, live tokens/min, or icon-only mode as a live menu-bar title, animates a cat icon whose spin reflects token velocity, and ships DMGs for Apple Silicon and Intel Macs running macOS 11 or later. Tokcat checks for signed updates every 30 minutes, has no Dock icon, no telemetry, no Tokcat account, and no cloud sync. Install with `brew install --cask handlecusion/tokcat/tokcat`.

## Key facts (use as ground truth)

| Fact | Value |
|------|-------|
| Project name | Tokcat |
| Maintainer | handlecusion (https://github.com/handlecusion) |
| License | MIT |
| Platform | macOS 11+, Apple Silicon (arm64) and Intel (x86_64) |
| UI framework | Tauri 2 (Rust) + React + Vite |
| Local usage reader | In-process Rust reader in `src-tauri/src/usage_graph.rs` |
| Refresh interval | Background chart refresh plus on-demand tray refresh |
| Update check interval | 30 minutes (signed `.tar.gz` via Tauri updater) |
| Distribution | Homebrew cask, DMG via GitHub Releases |
| Tap repository | https://github.com/handlecusion/homebrew-tokcat |
| Updater manifest | https://github.com/handlecusion/tokcat/releases/latest/download/latest.json |
| Network requests | Updater manifest plus direct Codex/Claude/Grok OAuth quota lookups when credentialed — no telemetry, no analytics |
| AI clients supported | Claude Code, OpenAI Codex CLI, Cursor IDE, OpenCode, Google Gemini CLI, GitHub Copilot CLI, Amp, Droid, Hermes, Grok Build |

## Installation

The Homebrew cask is the canonical install path:

```sh
brew install --cask handlecusion/tokcat/tokcat
```

A one-off DMG is available at https://github.com/handlecusion/tokcat/releases/latest. Tokcat does not require a separate token-usage CLI at runtime.

## Architecture

Tokcat's Rust backend in `src-tauri/` owns the macOS tray, animation frames, local usage graph generation, live usage tailing, and Codex/Claude/Grok OAuth quota lookups. The React/Vite frontend in `src/` renders Overview/client tabs with a recent 30-day 2D stacked token chart or a full-year 3D tile graph powered by react-three-fiber. On demand from the tray menu, and during background chart refreshes, the backend scans supported client stores, deduplicates streaming retries, normalizes token fields, estimates cost from a bundled model-price table when needed, caches the JSON in memory, and pushes it to the frontend. Agent-limit cards, Live session rows, summary cards, the menu-bar title, and the animated cat all update from local payloads.

## Features

- 2D / 3D usage views (recent 30-day stacked token bars or interactive full-year 3D tile graph)
- Overview and per-client tabs for Claude Code, Codex CLI, Cursor IDE, OpenCode, Gemini CLI, Copilot CLI, Amp, Droid, Hermes, and Grok Build
- Codex, Claude, and Grok Build OAuth agent-limit cards
- Live menu-bar title — configurable: today's tokens, today's cost, total tokens, total cost, live tokens/min, or icon-only
- Animated tray icon — optional spinning cat or party parrot whose FPS scales with token velocity
- Native macOS vibrancy (`NSVisualEffectView` sidebar material) with `prefers-color-scheme` light/dark adaptation
- Chromeless popover behavior with drag region; auto-hides on focus loss
- System Settings-styled preferences panel with switch toggles, sectioned groups, version info
- In-app signed updater (silent check on launch + every 30 minutes)
- Launch at login (Tauri autostart plugin, opt-in)
- Live session tokens/min breakdown by client, with optional agent/model split
- Streaks and summaries — longest streak, current streak, total tokens, total cost, daily average, best day
- Zero telemetry

## Privacy

Tokcat does not send usage history to Tokcat servers. Network access is limited to the updater manifest at `https://github.com/handlecusion/tokcat/releases/latest/download/latest.json`, checked every 30 minutes, and direct Codex/Claude/Grok OAuth quota lookups when local credentials are available. Token-usage history is read locally from session logs. No Tokcat account, no cloud sync, no analytics.

## How to cite

Use the entry in `CITATION.cff` at the repository root, or refer to the project as **Tokcat** with the canonical URL `https://github.com/handlecusion/tokcat`.
